title.source: How to archive a Go Vocal platform
title.target: How to archive a Go Vocal platform

body:0001.source: Why you may need to archive your platform
body:0001.target: Why you may need to archive your platform

body:0002.source: For compliance or legal reasons, you may need to archive your Go Vocal platform and store a copy of its content. This guide explains how to configure your archiving tool to work correctly with Go Vocal.
body:0002.target: For compliance or legal reasons, you may need to archive your Go Vocal platform and store a copy of its content. This guide explains how to configure your archiving tool to work correctly with Go Vocal.

body:0004.source: Go Vocal's frontend is a single-page application (SPA) built with JavaScript. Most web archiving tools use a scraper that visits every page and saves the HTML. However, many of these scrapers don't execute JavaScript — or don't execute it fully enough — which results in blank pages being saved.
body:0004.target: Go Vocal's frontend is a single-page application (SPA) built with JavaScript. Most web archiving tools use a scraper that visits every page and saves the HTML. However, many of these scrapers don't execute JavaScript — or don't execute it fully enough — which results in blank pages being saved.

body:0005.source: If this is the case with your scraping software, to solve this, Go Vocal provides a prerendering service that can serve fully rendered, static HTML to tools that identify themselves as crawlers. When the platform detects that a request comes from an archiving tool, it:
body:0005.target: If this is the case with your scraping software, to solve this, Go Vocal provides a prerendering service that can serve fully rendered, static HTML to tools that identify themselves as crawlers. When the platform detects that a request comes from an archiving tool, it:

body:0006.source: Serves a pure HTML version of the page instead of the JavaScript-based SPA.
body:0006.target: Serves a pure HTML version of the page instead of the JavaScript-based SPA.

body:0007.source: Bypasses the cookie consent manager, so the actual page content is visible without user interaction.
body:0007.target: Bypasses the cookie consent manager, so the actual page content is visible without user interaction.

body:0008.source: How to configure your archiving tool
body:0008.target: How to configure your archiving tool

body:0010.source: You can trigger the prerendered HTML version of the platform in one of two ways.
body:0010.target: You can trigger the prerendered HTML version of the platform in one of two ways.

body:0012.source: Configure your archiving tool to append _escaped_fragment_ as a query parameter to all your requests. For example:
body:0012.target: Configure your archiving tool to append _escaped_fragment_ as a query parameter to all your requests. For example:

body:0013.source: When the platform detects this parameter, it will serve a fully rendered HTML version of the page.
body:0013.target: When the platform detects this parameter, it will serve a fully rendered HTML version of the page.

body:0014.source: Option 2: Use a recognized User-Agent string
body:0014.target: Option 2: Use a recognized User-Agent string

body:0015.source: Make sure your archiving tool sends a User-Agent header that is recognized by Go Vocal. The following archiving tools are currently supported:
body:0015.target: Make sure your archiving tool sends a User-Agent header that is recognized by Go Vocal. The following archiving tools are currently supported:

body:0019.source: Heritrix (also used by Archive-It and many national libraries)
body:0019.target: Heritrix (also used by Archive-It and many national libraries)

body:0021.source: Internet Archive / Wayback Machine
body:0021.target: Internet Archive / Wayback Machine

body:0026.source: Bibliothèque nationale de France web archive
body:0026.target: Bibliothèque nationale de France web archive

body:0030.source: 💡 Tip: If your archiving software allows you to set a custom User-Agent string, setting it to include the name of one of the supported tools above is the easiest way to get it working.
body:0030.target: 💡 Tip: If your archiving software allows you to set a custom User-Agent string, setting it to include the name of one of the supported tools above is the easiest way to get it working.

body:0031.source: What if my tool isn't recognized?
body:0031.target: What if my tool isn't recognized?

body:0032.source: If your archiving tool uses a User-Agent string that is not in the list above and you cannot change it, use Option 1 instead — appending _escaped_fragment_ to your request URLs.
body:0032.target: If your archiving tool uses a User-Agent string that is not in the list above and you cannot change it, use Option 1 instead — appending _escaped_fragment_ to your request URLs.

body:0033.source: If neither option works for your setup, please contact support and we can look into adding your tool's User-Agent to our recognized list.
body:0033.target: If neither option works for your setup, please contact support and we can look into adding your tool's User-Agent to our recognized list.

body:0034.source: B. Covering all public facing content using /sitemap.xml
body:0034.target: B. Covering all public facing content using /sitemap.xml

body:0035.source: The platform contains some dynamic content, for example the “Load more” buttons in longer lists of projects or inputs. If your web archiving tool is configured to only act like a spider - this means it explores the platform merely by following internal links - the content behind these dynamic elements will not be discovered, as the buttons are not functional in the prerendered pages.
body:0035.target: The platform contains some dynamic content, for example the “Load more” buttons in longer lists of projects or inputs. If your web archiving tool is configured to only act like a spider - this means it explores the platform merely by following internal links - the content behind these dynamic elements will not be discovered, as the buttons are not functional in the prerendered pages.

body:0036.source: To solve this, make sure your archiving tool makes use of the /sitemap.xml file served from your platform. It lists all pages, to make the archive complete. Most scraping software will do this out of the box, but it’s worthwhile to check.
body:0036.target: To solve this, make sure your archiving tool makes use of the /sitemap.xml file served from your platform. It lists all pages, to make the archive complete. Most scraping software will do this out of the box, but it’s worthwhile to check.
